mei.relationship: Same mansion, same segment — both companies are energy-equal but non-complementary
mei.daily_feel: Zero communication barriers, but the partnership adds no clear value
mei.energy_flow_desc: Bidirectional and equal — no drain, but no elevation either
mei.long_term_risk: High risk of commoditized competition; information leakage damage exceeds partnership benefits
mei.growth: Low. Both companies are too similar to learn from each other
mei.stability: Medium. Stable but no growth driver; both take the hit simultaneously when markets shift
mei.bad_year_impact: In a bad year with shrinking markets, both fight over the same pie — partnership can instantly become rivalry
mei.fit_for: Partnerships with strict geographic market division or industry standard co-development

hr.relationship: Same mansion, same frequency — the partnership generates no additional value
hr.daily_feel: You understand their business, but they understand yours too — no information asymmetry advantage
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year both companies take simultaneous hits; the partnership may become a liability
hr.fit_for: Only suitable for partnerships with strict geographic or product line division

gyo.relationship: Karmic drive — your company naturally pushes the other's development agenda
gyo.daily_feel: An industry mission-like sense of purpose; willingness to invest resources comes naturally
gyo.energy_flow_desc: Slight output from your side, but karmic alignment provides long-term returns
gyo.long_term_risk: The gyo-party company tends to over-invest in this partnership at the expense of other opportunities
gyo.growth: Medium. Industry influence accumulates through driving the other's development
gyo.stability: Medium-high. Karmic foundation provides baseline stability, but the gyo party needs spending discipline
gyo.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the sense of mission intensifies — more resources in, fewer visible returns
gyo.fit_for: Companies with a long-term industry positioning mindset

hr.relationship: The other company depends on your push in the value chain — a natural upstream-downstream dynamic
hr.daily_feel: The other company naturally follows your industry rhythm; partnership friction is low
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the other faces greater pressure; your investment returns are delayed
hr.fit_for: Suitable for partner companies with patience for long-term industry positioning

tai.relationship: Karmic receiver — the other company naturally provides resources and market support
tai.daily_feel: Feeling supported by an industry elder; market entry costs decrease
tai.energy_flow_desc: The other company provides slight output; your company is the beneficiary
tai.long_term_risk: Over-reliance on the other's market resources degrades your independent capability
tai.growth: Medium. Passive growth following the other's market rhythm
tai.stability: Medium-high. Karmic support provides a buffer, but it can't last forever
tai.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the other's buffer reduces impact, less than going alone
tai.fit_for: Companies in growth phase that need market resources

hr.relationship: Your company plays the supported role in the other's industry ecosystem
hr.daily_feel: The other company is willing to share resources; partnership ramps up quickly
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the buffer helps, but if the other is also hit, the buffer vanishes
hr.fit_for: Suitable for expanding companies that need industry resource support

ei.relationship: Mutual resource investment with synchronized market position elevation
ei.daily_feel: Clear bidirectional returns from the partnership; investment visibly pays off
ei.energy_flow_desc: You actively invest market resources; the other's reciprocation prevents loss feelings
ei.long_term_risk: The ei party invests more; if reciprocation stops, a sudden partnership value gap appears
ei.growth: High. Bidirectional elevation — both market coverage and brand value grow
ei.stability: Medium-high. Stable while the positive cycle continues; quick imbalance if reciprocation breaks
ei.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the positive cycle may weaken but won't reverse into hostile competition
ei.fit_for: Companies willing to invest first and trust in long-term mutual benefit

hr.relationship: Positive cycle with the other company; both parties' market positions rise together
hr.daily_feel: Partnership results are quantifiable; both sides can see input-output ratios
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the positive cycle weakens but doesn't reverse
hr.fit_for: Suitable for strategically-minded partner companies willing to invest first

shin.relationship: The other company invests resources to cultivate the partnership; you're the supported party
shin.daily_feel: Feeling valued and invested in; the other party proactively provides market resources
shin.energy_flow_desc: The other party actively invests; your company benefits
shin.long_term_risk: Over-reliance on the other's resource investment; losing market competitiveness after separation
shin.growth: High. Passive but effective market growth; the key is internalizing capabilities
shin.stability: High. The shin position is a stable beneficiary role
shin.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the other's investment may decrease but won't reverse into competition
shin.fit_for: Companies that can internalize external resources and build independent capabilities

hr.relationship: The other company invests in cultivating the partnership; your company is the primary beneficiary
hr.daily_feel: The other party proactively provides resources and market support
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year investment effectiveness weakens but doesn't turn competitive
hr.fit_for: Suitable for partners that can internalize experience and gradually build independence

yu.relationship: Your company is the value exporter, continuously providing a comfortable partnership environment
yu.daily_feel: You proactively provide resources and support; the other enjoys them but may not notice the effort
yu.energy_flow_desc: Continuous output of market resources, technical support, and customer referrals; the other receives
yu.long_term_risk: The yu-party company's input-output ratio deteriorates year over year; partnership value is one-sidedly consumed
yu.growth: Medium. Market influence grows, but core capability deepening depends on yourself
yu.stability: Medium. The yu party's dissatisfaction accumulates; periodic reassessment of terms is needed
yu.bad_year_impact: In a bad year your resources tighten; the cost of maintaining the partnership feels heavier
yu.fit_for: Companies with sufficient resources for long-term investment and a clear stop-loss threshold

hr.relationship: Your company is the primary resource provider; the other benefits but reciprocation is limited
hr.daily_feel: The other company is accommodating but low-initiative; you're driving the partnership forward
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year your resource constraints make partnership maintenance pressure heavier
hr.fit_for: Suitable for partnerships where the provider has sufficient resources and accepts asymmetric returns

sui.relationship: The other company is the yu party investing heavily; you're the sui party being passively drained
sui.daily_feel: Resources and market opportunities come in, but your company's dependency keeps growing
sui.energy_flow_desc: The other invests heavily; your independent capability declines year over year
sui.long_term_risk: By the time you want to exit, you discover you can't function without the other's resources
sui.growth: High but passive. The other drives your market growth, but you don't choose the direction
sui.stability: Medium-low. Dependency deepens over time; exit costs keep rising
sui.bad_year_impact: In a bad year your independence is already depleted; if the other reduces support, you're in trouble
sui.fit_for: Companies with a clear exit timeline that are simultaneously building independent capabilities

hr.relationship: Your company gradually loses autonomy, becoming dependent on the other's resources and market
hr.daily_feel: Resources come in but your company's capacity is limited; long-term dependency risk is high
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus high dependency means the other can unilaterally change terms
hr.fit_for: Short-term partnerships are fine; long-term requires simultaneous capability building

an.relationship: Your company leads partnership stability, continuously outputting energy to maintain the framework
an.daily_feel: Like constantly maintaining partnership infrastructure; the other depends on your steady investment
an.energy_flow_desc: Continuous resource output to keep the partnership running; the other draws security from it
an.long_term_risk: The an-party company drains without realizing it; by the time resources are exhausted, it's too late
an.growth: Medium. Partnership management skills grow, but you're locked into the stabilizer role
an.stability: Surface-level high, actual drain. Stable when you're there; collapses when you can't sustain it
an.bad_year_impact: In a bad year, stabilization pressure doubles — potentially the straw that breaks your company's back
an.fit_for: Companies with strong partner relationship management that can set resource investment ceilings

hr.relationship: Your company is the partnership's stability anchor, but maintenance costs are underestimated
hr.daily_feel: The other thinks the partnership runs smoothly while your company silently absorbs maintenance costs
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year stabilization pressure doubles; risk of partnership collapse or your company being drained
hr.fit_for: Only suitable for partners with sufficient resources who can set investment limits

kai.relationship: The other company continuously extracts value from yours — 'nothing else is bearable'
kai.daily_feel: Subtle at first; after six months you notice partnership terms steadily worsening
kai.energy_flow_desc: The other absorbs your technology, customers, and market resources; your competitiveness flows out one-way
kai.long_term_risk: After two to three years you discover core competencies have already been transferred to the other party
kai.growth: Low. Resources are being extracted; no surplus for self-investment
kai.stability: Low. The kai position is the most unstable; the other can walk away with your resources at any time
kai.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus kai position means the partnership may rupture outright, and you've already lost core capabilities
kai.fit_for: Not recommended unless there are strict IP protections and exit clauses

hr.relationship: The other company continuously extracts core value; technology transfer is one-directional
hr.daily_feel: After six months the other starts demanding more; terms deteriorate steadily
hr.bad_year_impact: Bad year plus kai position means extremely high partnership rupture risk with irreversible losses
hr.fit_for: Not recommended without strict IP protection mechanisms

ki.relationship: The other company applies competitive pressure that drives innovation — a pressure-type partnership
ki.daily_feel: Competitive pressure is stimulating short-term but fatiguing long-term
ki.energy_flow_desc: The other applies pressure; your company absorbs it. Drain isn't negligible but has growth returns
ki.long_term_risk: The ki-party company's pressure tolerance has limits; exceeded, healthy competition becomes toxic drain
ki.growth: High but with costs. Pressure-driven innovation needs resource replenishment
ki.stability: Medium. Stable pressure is good stimulation; excessive pressure collapses the partnership
ki.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the pressure may crush rather than drive; the partnership becomes a burden
ki.fit_for: Companies with enough buffer resources to absorb pressure and innovate through competition

hr.relationship: The other company provides sustained competitive pressure driving your innovation
hr.daily_feel: Short-term innovation stimulus from the other, but watch for long-term fatigue
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year the pressure may overwhelm the partnership; needs monitoring
hr.fit_for: Suitable for partners with sufficient buffer resources that can innovate under pressure

sei.relationship: Your company grows through the other's pressure; partnership efficiency improves after alignment
sei.daily_feel: Lots of process conflicts initially; after adaptation, partnership efficiency visibly improves
sei.energy_flow_desc: The other's operating methods refine yours; passive strengthening with low drain
sei.long_term_risk: The sei party's risk is low, but initial adaptation and process integration costs are needed
sei.growth: High. Post-alignment growth is solid capability improvement
sei.stability: High. The sei position is one of the most stable partnership positions after alignment
sei.bad_year_impact: In a bad year friction intensifies but the partnership won't collapse
sei.fit_for: Companies that can tolerate initial process conflicts and value long-term partnership returns

hr.relationship: Your company grows through partnership friction; efficiency improves steadily after adaptation
hr.daily_feel: The other company needs initial adaptation, but post-alignment performance is stable
hr.bad_year_impact: In a bad year friction intensifies but won't cause partnership collapse
hr.fit_for: Suitable for partners that tolerate initial friction and focus on long-term value
